summary.intent: Address 5 Codex review findings (P1/P2) across 4 input components: PasswordInput, SearchInput, NumberInput, and PinInput — fixing missing prop-sync events, a stepping baseline bug, a roving tabindex accessibility issue, and a readonly guard gap.
summary.outcome: All 5 findings fixed across all affected components (not just the flagged ones), 18 new tests added (1959 total passing), clippy clean, all 10 xci-fast gates passed, specs updated, and changes committed.

repo: The codebase uses a state-machine pattern with `Event`, `Context`, and `transition` functions per input component — each component lives in its own mod.rs under crates/ars-components/src/input/<component>/mod.rs.

Prop-sync is implemented via `on_props_changed`/`SetProps`/`SetValue` event arms in the transition match — this pattern is required for controlled-mode correctness and was systematically missing from multiple components.
Snapshot tests are used for HTML output verification; insta snapshots must be explicitly accepted with a CLI command when intentional changes are made.
The xci-fast gate is the final CI check — 10 gates that must all pass before committing.
Specs live under spec/components/input/ as markdown files and must be kept in sync with implementation event sets.
PinInput uses roving tabindex for keyboard navigation — the invariant is that cell 0 always holds tabindex=0 when no cell is focused, preventing keyboard users from being stranded.

code.finding: Stepping from an empty value with default min=-∞ produced -inf; fix uses 0.0 clamped to [min, max] as the baseline when value is None.
Event::Clear was not guarded by the readonly check — added Clear to the readonly early-return guard alongside other mutation events.
input_attrs tabindex logic: when focused_index is None, cell 0 must return tabindex=0 (not -1) to preserve keyboard reachability.
Context::has_description was initialized false with no event to flip it; fixed by adding Event::SetHasDescription(bool) and the corresponding transition arm.

workflow: Codex review findings should be treated as a complete class fix — if a pattern is broken in the flagged component, audit all sibling components for the same defect rather than fixing only the reported instance.
Running tests synchronously (direct cargo test invocation) was more reliable than background task polling for getting immediate pass/fail feedback.
Clippy must be run after all edits are complete — lint errors (unused variables, redundant patterns) accumulated across the multi-file edit session and were cleaned up in a single pass at the end.
Snapshot diffs should be diffed and verified before accepting — confirmed only the intended tabindex change (cell 0: -1 → 0 when unfocused) appeared before running the accept command.

friction: Test output required multiple bash invocation attempts (background task polling, then direct synchronous run) before results were reliably readable — the async task output mechanism added friction to the tight edit-compile-test loop.
Clippy lint errors accumulated silently across the multi-file editing session and only surfaced at the final clippy run, requiring a cleanup pass over multiple files.
open_items: Codex flagged only PasswordInput for missing prop-sync events but the same gap existed in SearchInput, NumberInput, and PinInput — worth verifying no other components outside the input/ directory share this class of defect.

The has_description context flag pattern (initialized false, flipped by event) may exist in other components beyond PasswordInput — a systematic audit of Context structs for similar unflippable boolean fields could be worthwhile.
